Study Statement

A quiet touch can carry a clear intention.

My study of piano begins with listening: to silence before the first note, to the shape of a phrase, and to the space between intensity and control.

I am currently studying classical music, chamber collaboration, and the expressive detail of live performance.

Touch Study

A personal detail from the keyboard.

The hand learns to listen before it speaks. I study touch as a way of shaping silence, weight, and color.

Selected Study Recordings

Two protected study recordings for listening.

Shared as restrained listening documents, with reduced visual quality and watermark protection.

Composers in Study

Listening to different musical languages.

01

S. Rachmaninoff

Color, breadth, and emotional tension

02

L. v. Beethoven

Structure, rhythm, and inner strength

03

F. Chopin

Touch, line, and poetic detail

04

Debussy, Haydn, Bach

Sound color, clarity, and counterpoint

Interpretation Notes

Tone, silence, and structure are studied as one breath.

Tone

I look for a sound that can remain clear without becoming loud.

Phrase

A phrase should feel spoken, with direction before beauty.

Form

I study form as quiet architecture, not as something separate from emotion.

"

Music is not decoration. It is a way of giving form to breath, memory, discipline, and light.

Current Study

Piano Major

Current studies in solo piano, theory, chamber music, ear training, and performance practice.

tone before volume

clarity before speed

silence before sound
